goal: Wire the `review_changes` MCP tool into `McpServer::handle_tools_call` so it fans out G4 (architecture blast-radius), G7 (quality + security via the existing `execute_g7` backbone) and G8 (untested-functions via `execute_g8`) IN PARALLEL over a caller-supplied `changed_files` list, then projects the merged results into a unified, severity-ranked `findings[]` array with per-finding `source_group` provenance plus `blast_radius` and `untested_functions` sub-objects per master-plan §3.2 row 13 (`Analyze diff/PR against conventions, quality, security, tests, blast radius`).
plan_summary: Solo-feature follow-on to WO-0090 (which shipped `handle_check_quality` consuming G7+G8) and WO-0083 (which shipped `handle_blast_radius` consuming G4). `review_changes` is the COMPOSITION tool: it does NOT introduce a new G-source backbone — it reuses the EXISTING `execute_g4` / `execute_g7` / `execute_g8` orchestrators by fanning all three out in a single `tokio::join!(...)` call, then sorting their merged outputs into a single severity-ranked `findings[]` array (Critical → High → Medium → Low). New `pub async fn handle_review_changes(&self, id: &Value, params: &Value) -> Value` lives adjacent to `handle_check_quality` at `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs:~1870` (placement preserves the existing G7/G8 → G4 ordering of doc-cross-references). Dispatch-chain ELSE-IF branch added in `handle_tools_call` AT `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs:~961` (BEFORE the existing `check_quality` branch at line 963 since `review_changes` is a composition over `check_quality`'s sources plus G4) — preserves the existing `if/else if` shape per WO-0090 §critic check-5 precedent. Frozen test `test_review_changes_tool` at MODULE ROOT of `server.rs` per DEC-0007 + WO-0090 + WO-0089 + WO-0085 precedent (now 8 WOs deep). The test seeds TestG4Source + TestG7Source + TestG8Source (each one a DEC-0008 §4 dependency-inversion seam, carry-forward from WO-0083 / WO-0085 / WO-0089 — now 6+ WOs deep), constructs an `McpServer` via `.with_g4_sources(...).with_g7_sources(...).with_g8_sources(...)`, dispatches a `tools/call` JSON-RPC payload `{name: "review_changes", arguments: {changed_files: ["src/foo.rs", "src/bar.py"], reason: "verifier smoke"}}`, and asserts SA1-SA8: (SA1) `findings[]` length == 5 (3 G7 issues + 2 G4 blast-radius nodes projected as Medium-severity findings + 0 G8 — see scope_in #6 for the seed shape); (SA2) `findings[]` is sorted descending by severity with `findings[0].severity == "critical"`; (SA3) `findings[].source_group` distribution covers `{quality, architecture}`; (SA4) `untested_functions[]` length == 2; (SA5) `blast_radius.impacted[]` length == 2; (SA6) `meta.master_timed_out == false`; (SA7) `meta.wall_elapsed_ms < 6000` ms (parallelism wall-clock canary tuned to `< sum-of-per-source-deadlines / 2`; sequential awaits would exceed this); (SA8) finding shape (`{severity, category, source_group, file, line, message}`) is fully populated for every entry. Mutation contract M1+M2+M3+M4 (one mutation per G-source bypass + one filter-bypass) targeting the parallel fan-out + severity-rank invariant. All four reversible via `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` + md5sum verify per WO-0067/0083/0085/0089/0090 protocol.
scope_in: 1) Add `pub async fn handle_review_changes(&self, id: &Value, params: &Value) -> Value` to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs`, placed adjacent to `handle_check_quality` (line ~1870; insert AFTER `handle_check_quality` — preserves doc-cross-reference ordering G4 → G7 → G8 → composition). The handler MUST: (a) parse `changed_files: Vec<String>` (required, list of file paths — JSON-RPC `-32602` if missing/non-array); parse `reason: String` (required per CEQP — JSON-RPC `-32602` if missing/non-string); accept optional `current_task` / `files_in_context` / `token_budget` CEQP fields (accepted but not surfaced); (b) construct `g4_query: G4Query { changed_nodes: changed_files (parsed as PathBuf seed list), max_blast_depth: BLAST_RADIUS_DEFAULT_MAX_DEPTH, max_edges: BLAST_RADIUS_DEFAULT_MAX_EDGES }`, `g7_query: G7Query { target: changed_files[0].clone() (use first file as anchor, document inline), categories: vec![] }`, `g8_query: G8Query { changed_files: changed_files (full list, not just first) }`; (c) fan out `execute_g4` + `execute_g7` + `execute_g8` IN PARALLEL via `tokio::join!(...)` (3-arity); (d) call `merge_g4_dependency_union(&g4_outcome.results, &g4_query)` (returns `Vec<G4UnifiedEdge>`), `merge_g7_by_severity(&all_g7_issues)` (filter to Available status first per `handle_check_quality` precedent), `merge_g8_test_discoveries(&g8_outcome)`; (e) project each G7 issue + each G4 blast-radius node into a `Finding { severity, category, source_group, file, line, message }` (G4 nodes get severity=`medium`, category=`blast_radius`, source_group=`architecture`; G7 issues retain their native severity, category, and get source_group=`quality`); (f) sort the combined `findings[]` array descending by severity weight (Critical=4, High=3, Medium=2, Low=1, Info=0; ties broken by source_group then by file; document the weight constants inline at point-of-use); (g) project the canonical wire shape `{ findings: [{ severity, category, source_group, file, line, message }], blast_radius: { impacted: project_blast_radius_impacted(merged_g4, &target_seeds) (REUSE the helper from handle_blast_radius), dependency_chain: build_dependency_chains(merged_g4, &target_seeds) (REUSE) }, untested_functions: [{ test_path, source_path, methods_found_by, max_confidence }], meta: { master_timed_out: g4_outcome.master_timed_out || g7_outcome.master_timed_out || g8_outcome.master_timed_out, wall_elapsed_ms } }`; (h) return JSON-RPC `Value` with `result.content[0].text = serde_json::to_string(&payload).unwrap_or_else(|_| summary.clone())` (degraded textual fallback per WO-0090 §executor lesson on production-side `unwrap_or_else`). The handler MUST NOT panic on empty G4/G7/G8 source lists — return an empty `findings[]` / `untested_functions[]` / `blast_radius.impacted[]` arrays.

2) Wire `review_changes` into the `handle_tools_call` `if/else if name == "..."` dispatch chain in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` at line ~961 (POSITION: BEFORE the existing `check_quality` branch at line 963 since `review_changes` composes G4 + G7 + G8 and the chain reads top-down — review_changes is logically a SUPERSET of check_quality, place it FIRST). Match-condition: `if name == "review_changes" && (self.g4_sources.is_some() || self.g7_sources.is_some() || self.g8_sources.is_some())` — fall through to the phase-1 stub path when ALL three source slots are `None` (preserves phase-1 invariant #9 — see WO-0083 / WO-0085 / WO-0090 dispatch-chain shape). The chain MUST remain `if/else if` (NO `match` rewrite — preserves diff size + critic check-5 Public API precedent).

3) `McpServer` struct does NOT gain new fields — `g4_sources` (Arc<Vec<Arc<dyn G4Source>>>), `g7_sources` (Arc<Vec<Arc<dyn G7Source>>>) and `g8_sources` (Arc<Vec<Arc<dyn G8Source>>>) ALREADY exist (WO-0083 / WO-0085 / WO-0089 shipped them). The existing `with_g4_sources`, `with_g7_sources`, `with_g8_sources` builder methods are sufficient. NO new builder methods required. NO new fields required. This is what makes `review_changes` a NATURAL solo-feature WO — composition-only, ZERO field surface change to `McpServer`.

4) DEC-0007 frozen-test-at-module-root: `test_review_changes_tool` lives as `#[cfg(test)] #[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 4)] async fn test_review_changes_tool() { ... }` at MODULE ROOT of `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests {}`). The substring selector `cargo test -p ucil-daemon server::test_review_changes_tool` MUST resolve uniquely from a `cargo clean` baseline. Acceptance test selector in `feature-list.json` is `cargo_test -p ucil-daemon server::test_review_changes_tool` — the test name MUST match this selector verbatim.

5) `test_review_changes_tool` MUST seed THREE deterministic in-process G-sources via DEC-0008 §4 dependency-inversion seam: (a) `TestG4Source` returning a `G4SourceOutput` with EXACTLY 2 `G4UnifiedEdge` entries seeded from `changed_files[0]` so `project_blast_radius_impacted` yields 2 impacted nodes (model on the WO-0083 `test_blast_radius_tool` seed pattern); (b) `TestG7Source` returning `G7SourceOutput { status: Available, issues: vec![Critical, High, Medium] }` with 3 distinct issues spanning Critical/High/Medium severities (model on the WO-0090 `test_check_quality_tool` seed); (c) `TestG8Source` returning `G8SourceOutput` with EXACTLY 2 `G8TestCandidate` entries (test_path / source_path / methods distinct, model on WO-0090 `test_check_quality_tool` seed). Each carry-forward seed is now 6+ WOs deep — implement ALL trait methods (no `unimplemented!()` stubs) per WO-0090 §executor lesson on test-side trait carry-forward integrity.

6) `test_review_changes_tool` MUST: (a) construct the three seeded sources from #5; (b) build an `McpServer` via `McpServer::new().with_g4_sources(Arc::new(vec![Arc::new(g4_src) as Arc<dyn G4Source>])).with_g7_sources(...).with_g8_sources(...)`; (c) construct a `tools/call` JSON-RPC payload `{ "jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": <some>, "method": "tools/call", "params": { "name": "review_changes", "arguments": { "changed_files": ["src/foo.rs", "src/bar.py"], "reason": "verifier smoke" } } }`; (d) call `server.handle_line(&payload).await`; (e) parse `response.result.content[0].text` as JSON and assert SA1-SA8: SA1 — `findings[]` length == 5 (3 G7 issues + 2 G4 blast-radius nodes; G8 provides `untested_functions[]` not `findings[]` per scope_in #1.e); SA2 — `findings[0].severity == "critical"` AND `findings[]` is sorted descending (assert weight(findings[i].severity) >= weight(findings[i+1].severity) for all i); SA3 — `findings[].source_group` set (collected via `BTreeSet`) covers AT LEAST `{"quality", "architecture"}`; SA4 — `untested_functions[]` length == 2; SA5 — `blast_radius.impacted[]` length == 2; SA6 — `meta.master_timed_out == false`; SA7 — `meta.wall_elapsed_ms < 6000` (parallelism wall-clock canary; sequential awaits over 3 G-sources with G4=12s, G7=5.5s, G8=5s masters would compound past 6000 ms — wall-clock guard catches sequential-await regressions per WO-0090 SA6 precedent); SA8 — for each entry in `findings[]`, every required field (`severity`, `category`, `source_group`, `file`, `line`, `message`) is present and non-null AND `severity ∈ {"critical", "high", "medium", "low", "info"}` (lowercase per §5.7 + §12.1 vocabulary canary). Each `assert!` / `assert_eq!` MUST carry the `(SAn) <semantic name>; left: ...; right: ...` panic-body shape per DEC-0007 + WO-0067 mature pattern.

7) Mutation contract M1 + M2 + M3 + M4 (4 mutations on the SINGLE feature, all in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs`): M1 (G4-route bypass) — patch the `tokio::join!(execute_g4(...), execute_g7(...), execute_g8(...))` 3-arity macro so the G4 arm becomes `let g4_outcome = G4UnionOutcome::default(); /* M1 BYPASS */ ; let (g7_outcome, g8_outcome) = tokio::join!(execute_g7(...), execute_g8(...))` (or simpler: clear `g4_outcome.results` after the join via `g4_outcome.results.clear()` per WO-0090 verifier-side simplification precedent). Trips SA5 (`(SA5) blast_radius.impacted[] length; left: 0, right: 2`). M2 (G7-route bypass) — symmetric — clear `all_g7_issues` between merge stages OR replace G7 join arm with empty Default. Trips SA1 + SA3 (`(SA1) findings[] length; left: 2, right: 5` AND/OR `(SA3) source_group set missing quality`). M3 (G8-route bypass) — symmetric — clear `merged_candidates` between projection stages OR replace G8 join arm with empty Default. Trips SA4 (`(SA4) untested_functions[] length; left: 0, right: 2`). M4 (severity-rank bypass) — patch the `findings.sort_by(...)` call to `findings.sort_by(|_, _| std::cmp::Ordering::Equal)` (a no-op compare that preserves arbitrary insertion order). Trips SA2 (`(SA2) findings[0].severity; left: ...; right: "critical"`) when G4 medium-severity nodes are inserted before the G7 Critical issue in the unsorted concat. All four reversible via `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` + md5sum verify against `/tmp/wo-0092-server-orig.md5sum` snapshot per WO-0067 / WO-0090 protocol. Per WO-0090 §verifier lesson on mutation-patch simplification: verifier MAY apply minimal-equivalent `.clear()` forms in place of struct-replacement forms when `#![deny(warnings)]` would reject the latter; document in the verification report's `## Disclosed deviations` section.

8) `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` re-export block: ADD `pub use server::{ReviewChangesResponse, ReviewFindingWire, BlastRadiusBriefWire};` ONLY IF the executor introduces serde-derived public wire types. Per WO-0090 §planner lesson on wire-type re-exports being over-prescribed: if the executor projects via `serde_json::json!(...)` directly inline (matching the `handle_blast_radius` line 1809-1832 inline-`json!` pattern), NO new public types are needed and NO new re-exports required. Executor judgment per WO-0090 W3 critic warning rationale.

9) `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.tool.review_changes")]` on `handle_review_changes` per master-plan §15.2 line 1519. NO numeric-cast `fields(...)` per WO-0085 §planner lesson — let tracing auto-capture fn args. Inside the handler body, span-attach the `changed_files` argument via `tracing::Span::current().record("changed_files_count", changed_files.len() as i64)` AFTER parsing (count, not the array — keeps span field cardinality bounded per §15.2).

10) Word-ban scrub MUST cover the production-side of `server.rs` (NOT the `#[cfg(test)]` carve-out). Pre-flight grep BEFORE first push: `grep -niE 'mock|fake|stub' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs | grep -v '#\[cfg(test)\]' | head -20` — production-side hits MUST be empty. Test-side `ScriptedFakeSerenaClient` / `TestG4Source` / `TestG7Source` / `TestG8Source` are exempt under DEC-0008 §4 dependency-inversion seam.

11) Module-coherence per DEC-0005: BUNDLE in ONE feat commit (single feature; no pair to split): feat — `handle_review_changes` body + dispatch-chain wiring + frozen `test_review_changes_tool` test + (optional) lib.rs re-exports for any wire types. NO splits like `handler-without-test` or `dispatch-without-handler` — they produce stub-shaped intermediate states critic flags. Commit cadence per WO-0090 §planner-lesson alignment: 4 commits = 1 feat + 1 test (separate per DEC-0005 carve-out for the long frozen test) + 1 refactor (if any AC tightening needed) + 1 RFR.

12) Pre-emptively carve out `ucil-build/verification-reports/**` AND `ucil-build/escalations/**` from `forbidden_paths` (see WO-0067 scope_in #42 standing template through WO-0090). The phase-1/2/3 effectiveness-evaluator + gate-check child agents commit refresh artefacts during gate execution.

13) Standing-protocol coverage workaround per WO-0058..WO-0090 (now 43+ WOs deep): the `coverage-gate.sh` sccache `RUSTC_WRAPPER` interaction reports `cargo llvm-cov errored` for `ucil-core` / `ucil-embeddings` / sometimes `ucil-daemon`. Skip the `[FAIL] coverage gate: ucil-{core,embeddings,daemon}` lines and report the AC22 standing-protocol substantive measurement via `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-daemon --summary-only --json | jq '.data[0].totals.lines.percent'`. The actual `ucil-daemon` coverage MUST be ≥ 85% per AC22.

14) AC selectors must tolerate rustfmt macro-arg wrapping per WO-0090 §planner lesson: AC10 (parallel fan-out evidence) is written as `grep -E -A 6 'tokio::join!' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs | grep -E 'execute_g4|execute_g7|execute_g8'` (multi-line tolerant; rustfmt wraps 3-arity `tokio::join!(...)` across multiple lines).

15) Lessons-applied (carry forward through WO-0090): (a) DEC-0007 module-root frozen tests (now 8+ WOs deep through WO-0090 for daemon-side tests); (b) DEC-0008 §4 dependency-inversion seam for TestG4/G7/G8Source (now 6+ WOs deep); (c) WO-0085 §planner 'drop tracing fields(...) numeric casts'; (d) WO-0086 §planner 'AC commit-cadence vs scope_in cohesion alignment' — this WO prescribes 4 commits = 1 feat + 1 test + 1 refactor + 1 RFR (single-feature variant of the WO-0090 5-6 commit template); (e) WO-0090 §executor 'always RFR-disclose `.unwrap()` / `.expect()` shipped in production code'; (f) WO-0090 §planner 'multi-line tolerant grep for `tokio::join!`'; (g) WO-0090 §planner 'verify dep-graph claims via cargo metadata at WO-emission time' — see scope_out #4 below for the dep-graph audit; (h) tracing carve-out for pure-deterministic CPU-bound functions does NOT apply here — `handle_review_changes` is async/IO so DOES carry `#[tracing::instrument]`.
scope_out: 1) Production-wiring of REAL `LspDiagnosticsG7Source` / `EslintG7Source` / `RuffG7Source` / `SemgrepG7Source` / `ConventionTestG8Source` / `ImportTestG8Source` / `KgRelationsTestG8Source` / `CodeGraphContextG4Source` / `LSPCallHierarchyG4Source` impls into daemon startup orchestration (`lifecycle.rs` boot-time wiring, `McpServer::with_default_g4_g7_g8_sources()` factory). This is the SAME deferral as WO-0083 G4 / WO-0085 G7 / WO-0089 G8 / WO-0090 backbone WOs — production-wiring follow-up WO bundles the daemon-startup orchestration registration with the real source impls. F11 acceptance only requires the dispatch handler + test-source-injected integration test.

2) Convention-side findings via G3 (knowledge graph). Per master-plan §3.2 row 13, `review_changes` SHOULD include conventions in the analysis. However, G3 (Knowledge) does NOT have a parallel-execute backbone yet — only `handle_get_conventions` (KG read path) is shipped. The `findings[]` array CAN be extended with G3 convention findings in a future WO once an `execute_g3_for_review` parallel orchestrator lands. For F11 acceptance the analysis spans G4 (architecture/blast-radius) + G7 (quality+security) + G8 (untested-functions); convention findings is OUT OF SCOPE.

3) Feedback loop post-hoc analyser (P3-W11-F12) — different architectural axis (post-response session-state mutation in `ucil-core`, NOT MCP tool dispatch in `ucil-daemon`). Subsequent solo-feature WO.
4) Warm processors (P3-W10-F13) — different architectural axis (rule-based scheduled jobs in `ucil-daemon::agent_scheduler`, NOT MCP tool dispatch in `server.rs`). Subsequent WO.

5) Integration test suites P3-W9-F11 (incremental), P3-W11-F13 (quality pipeline), P3-W11-F14 (testing pipeline), P3-W11-F16 (full query pipeline) — different architectural axis (additive `tests/integration/test_*.rs` files using docker fixtures). Subsequent WO bundles 2-4 of these per the test-suite-bundle template.

6) Adding new workspace-level Cargo deps. Dep-graph audit at WO-emission time (per WO-0090 §planner lesson): `cargo metadata --manifest-path crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml --format-version 1 | jq -r '.packages[] | select(.name == "ucil-daemon") | .dependencies[].name'` confirms `ucil-lsp-diagnostics` is already a `ucil-daemon` dep (added by WO-0090). The G4Source / G7Source / G8Source traits + execute_g4 / execute_g7 / execute_g8 / merge_g4_dependency_union / merge_g7_by_severity / merge_g8_test_discoveries / project_blast_radius_impacted / build_dependency_chains helpers ARE ALREADY in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g4.rs` + `g7.rs` + `g8.rs` + `server.rs` (WO-0083 / WO-0085 / WO-0089 / WO-0090). NO new deps needed.

7) Modifying any pre-existing `handle_*` body in `server.rs` (`handle_check_quality`, `handle_blast_radius`, `handle_get_architecture`, `handle_type_check`, `handle_find_definition`, etc.). This WO is purely ADDITIVE — only NEW handler function `handle_review_changes` and dispatch-chain ELSE-IF branch addition.
8) `match name` rewrite of the dispatch chain. The chain remains `if/else if` per WO-0083 / WO-0085 / WO-0089 / WO-0090 critic check-5 Public API precedent.

9) Standing carry-forwards (NOT new debt): coverage-gate.sh sccache `RUSTC_WRAPPER` workaround (now 43+ WOs deep — Bucket B / Bucket D triage candidate); reality-check.sh pre-existing-stash bug (now 17+ WOs deep — verifier MUST treat as informational only per WO-0067 §verifier lesson, M1+M2+M3+M4 in-place mutation contract is the authoritative anti-laziness layer); effectiveness-gate.sh claude-p sub-session timeout flake (3 open escalations); 'no merge commits introduced on feat' via `git log feat ^main --merges = 0`.
10) `attempts >= 10` features blocked at the planner level — F11 has `attempts: 0` per the feature-list snapshot at WO-execution time.
acceptance: F11: `cargo test -p ucil-daemon server::test_review_changes_tool` exits 0 from a `cargo clean` baseline.

F11: The frozen test asserts SA1-SA8 covering finding count + severity-rank invariant + source-group provenance distribution + blast_radius/untested_functions sub-objects + meta wall-clock canary + finding-shape integrity.
F11: Mutation M1 (drop G4 from parallel join) flips PASS → FAIL with `(SA5) blast_radius.impacted[] length; left: 0, right: 2` (or equivalent SA1 finding-count regression).
F11: Mutation M2 (drop G7 from parallel join) flips PASS → FAIL with a SA1 finding-count or SA3 source_group regression.
F11: Mutation M3 (drop G8 from parallel join) flips PASS → FAIL with `(SA4) untested_functions[] length; left: 0, right: 2`.
F11: Mutation M4 (severity-rank no-op compare) flips PASS → FAIL with `(SA2) findings[0].severity` regression.
F11: Production-side word-ban scrub (`grep -niE 'mock|fake|stub' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs | grep -v '#\[cfg(test)\]'`) returns empty.
F11: AC22 standing-protocol substantive coverage on `ucil-daemon` ≥ 85% via `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-daemon --summary-only --json | jq '.data[0].totals.lines.percent'`.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: cargo test -p ucil-daemon server::test_review_changes_tool exits 0 from a cargo clean baseline
AC2: cargo build -p ucil-daemon exits 0
AC3: cargo clippy -p ucil-daemon -- -D warnings exits 0
AC4: cargo fmt --check -p ucil-daemon exits 0

AC5: grep -nE '^[[:space:]]*(pub )?(async )?fn test_review_changes_tool\b' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs returns at least 1 line and the matched line is at MODULE ROOT (NOT under a 'mod tests' block — verify via line-number against nearest preceding 'mod tests {' or absence thereof)
AC6: grep -nE 'fn handle_review_changes\b' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs returns at least 1 line
AC7: grep -E '"review_changes"' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs returns at least 2 lines (one for the existing ToolDescriptor entry around line 339, one for the new dispatch-chain branch)
AC8: grep -nE '#\[tracing::instrument\(name = "ucil\.tool\.review_changes"\)\]' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs returns at least 1 line
AC9: grep -nE 'tracing::Span::current\(\)\.record\("changed_files_count"' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs returns at least 1 line

AC10: grep -E -A 6 'tokio::join!' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs | grep -E 'execute_g4|execute_g7|execute_g8' returns at least 3 distinct matches (3-arity parallel fan-out evidence; multi-line tolerant per WO-0090 §planner lesson)

AC11: grep -nE 'merge_g4_dependency_union|merge_g7_by_severity|merge_g8_test_discoveries' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs returns at least 6 lines (helper-reuse evidence — 2 per merge fn × 3 fns: existing in handle_get_architecture/handle_blast_radius/handle_check_quality + new in handle_review_changes)

AC12: grep -nE 'project_blast_radius_impacted|build_dependency_chains' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs returns at least 4 lines (helper-reuse evidence — 2 per fn × 2 fns: existing in handle_blast_radius + new in handle_review_changes)

AC13: Production-side word-ban: grep -niE 'mock|fake|stub' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs | awk -F: '$2+0 < FIRST_TEST_LINE' (where FIRST_TEST_LINE is the first '#[cfg(test)]' line) returns empty — production code carries NO mock/fake/stub identifiers

AC14: M1 mutation (drop execute_g4 from join — replace `let (g4_outcome, g7_outcome, g8_outcome) = tokio::join!(...)` so G4 arm becomes a Default — OR `g4_outcome.results.clear()` post-join per WO-0090 verifier-side simplification precedent) makes test_review_changes_tool FAIL with `(SA5) blast_radius.impacted[] length` panic body OR an equivalent SA1 finding-count regression; restore via `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` + md5sum verify against /tmp/wo-0092-server-orig.md5sum
AC15: M2 mutation (drop execute_g7 from join — symmetric to M1 on the G7 side) makes test_review_changes_tool FAIL with a SA1 finding-count or SA3 source_group regression; restore + md5sum verify
AC16: M3 mutation (drop execute_g8 from join — symmetric on G8) makes test_review_changes_tool FAIL with `(SA4) untested_functions[] length; left: 0, right: 2`; restore + md5sum verify

AC17: M4 mutation (severity-rank bypass — patch `findings.sort_by(...)` to a no-op `Ordering::Equal` compare) makes test_review_changes_tool FAIL with `(SA2) findings[0].severity` regression (the unsorted concat puts G4 medium-severity nodes before the G7 Critical issue in insertion order); restore + md5sum verify
AC18: Frozen-test SA shape: grep -E '\(SA[1-8]\) ' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs returns ≥ 8 lines (one panic-body label per SA assertion per DEC-0007 + WO-0067 mature pattern)

AC19: Frozen-test parallelism wall-clock canary: SA7 asserts `meta.wall_elapsed_ms < 6000` — the literal `6000` MUST appear AT or NEAR the SA7 assertion site (grep -E 'SA7|wall_elapsed_ms' crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs returns evidence near the assertion)
